checklist.notes: IBMi green screen with RPG IV/COBOL programs, embedded business logic spanning 30+ years
HIPAA, HITECH, state health data privacy laws; PHI handling throughout modernization
DB2/400 to modern RDBMS migration with PHI data; referential integrity across decades of records
HL7/FHIR interfaces, claims clearinghouses, pharmacy systems, lab systems, state registries

checklist.item: User base size and change management
checklist.notes: ~200 clinical and administrative staff accustomed to green screen workflows for 15+ years
5250 terminal UI to modern web; RPG to modern API layer; monolith to service-oriented
Role-based access, MFA, audit trails, session management for clinical workflows
24/7 clinical operations; zero-downtime migration strategy required
IBMi/RPG specialists increasingly rare; dual-competency resources needed
Some workflows can be modernized as-is; others need redesign for web paradigm

complexity_factors: Legacy RPG IV/COBOL codebase with 30+ years of embedded business logic
HIPAA/HITECH compliance requirements for all data in transit and at rest
PHI data migration from DB2/400 requiring field-level encryption and audit trails
HL7 v2 and FHIR R4 integration with external healthcare systems
Zero-downtime cutover requirement for 24/7 clinical operations
Scarce IBMi/RPG talent pool necessitating specialized recruitment
Change management for 200+ users transitioning from 5250 green screen to web UI
Dual-run period requiring parallel system operation during validation

responsibilities: Overall system design and architecture governance
IBMi modernization strategy and pattern selection
Healthcare integration architecture (HL7/FHIR)
Well-Architected reviews and compliance alignment
Technical risk management and mitigation planning
Modern web UI development replacing 5250 green screens
API layer development and service decomposition
Database migration tooling and data transformation logic
Unit and integration test development
Frontend accessibility and responsive design (WCAG 2.1 AA)
RPG IV and COBOL program analysis and documentation
Business logic extraction and service wrapping
DB2/400 schema analysis and migration mapping
IBMi program call interfaces and API bridge development
Dual-run validation and legacy decommission support
Clinical workflow analysis and user research
Green screen to web UI interaction mapping
Wireframes, prototypes, and design system creation
Usability testing with clinical and administrative staff
Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508)
HIPAA security rule compliance architecture
Identity and access management design (RBAC, MFA)
PHI encryption strategy (at rest and in transit)
Audit trail and access logging implementation
Penetration testing coordination and vulnerability remediation
Test strategy and plan development
Automated regression test suite creation
Data migration validation and reconciliation testing
HIPAA compliance and security testing
User acceptance testing coordination
Performance and load testing
Agile ceremony facilitation and sprint planning
Stakeholder communication and status reporting
Risk and issue management
Budget tracking and resource allocation
Vendor and third-party coordination
Change management and training coordination
CI/CD pipeline design and implementation
Infrastructure as Code (Terraform/Ansible)
Container orchestration and deployment automation
Monitoring, logging, and alerting setup
HIPAA-compliant environment provisioning
Disaster recovery and backup automation

by_component.component: RPG/COBOL Business Logic Extraction & API Wrapping
DB2/400 Data Migration to Modern RDBMS

risk_buffer.rationale: High complexity IBMi modernization with HIPAA compliance, scarce RPG talent, and zero-downtime cutover requirement justify a 20% risk buffer. Legacy systems frequently surface undocumented business rules during extraction.

caveats: RPG program complexity will be refined during discovery phase code analysis
DB2/400 record counts and schema complexity are estimated from initial assessment
HL7 interface count based on architecture document; additional interfaces may surface
Hourly rates assume US-based senior talent; offshore mix could reduce cost 15-25%
refinement_triggers: Completion of RPG codebase static analysis
DB2/400 schema and data volume audit
Vendor selection for FHIR integration engine

optimization_strategies.description: Prioritize highest-traffic screens first; defer low-usage screens to Phase 2, reducing initial scope by ~15%
optimization_strategies.risk: Users on deferred screens continue using 5250 longer, increasing dual-run operational cost

optimization_strategies.strategy: RPG service wrapping over full rewrite
optimization_strategies.description: Wrap stable RPG programs as REST APIs via IBMi Integrated Web Services rather than rewriting in modern language
optimization_strategies.risk: Long-term maintenance debt; retained IBMi dependency for wrapped services

optimization_strategies.description: Use nearshore developers for UI modernization work while keeping IBMi specialists and architect onshore
optimization_strategies.risk: Timezone coordination overhead; HIPAA BAA requirements for offshore data access

optimization_strategies.description: Use HAPI FHIR (open-source) instead of commercial integration engine, reducing third-party licensing
optimization_strategies.risk: Increased DevOps burden for FHIR server management; no vendor SLA

optimization_strategies.strategy: Automated test generation from green screen recordings
optimization_strategies.description: Record existing 5250 session workflows to auto-generate regression test baselines, reducing manual QA effort
optimization_strategies.risk: Recording tooling may not capture all edge cases; manual review still required for PHI-related flows

assumptions: Client provides dedicated IBMi environment access for development and testing within 2 weeks of kickoff
Existing RPG IV source code is available and version-controlled; no object-only programs without source
Client IT staff available for 8-10 hours/week for knowledge transfer and legacy system documentation
HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) executed before any PHI data access
Cloud infrastructure provider supports HIPAA-eligible services (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government, or equivalent)
No more than 50 unique 5250 green screens require modernization in Phase 1
DB2/400 database contains fewer than 500 tables and 50M total records for initial migration scope
HL7 v2 interfaces number 8-12; FHIR R4 interfaces number 3-5
Client accepts 4-week dual-run period with rollback capability before legacy decommission
All team members pass HIPAA security training before engagement begins
Rates are based on US market averages for Q1 2026; subject to adjustment for specialized IBMi talent
Client has existing Active Directory or compatible IdP for SSO integration
